<content>1: // ─── WebSocket Message Router (Ticket 2.2) ──────────────────────────────────
2: // Pure logic for routing incoming WebSocket messages to correct handlers,
3: // client tracking, broadcast targeting, and state snapshot building.
4: // Deliberately IO-free: no actual WebSocket I/O — just routing decisions.
5: 
6: import type { RelayMessage } from "./types.js";
7: 
8: // ─── Message types from browser ──────────────────────────────────────────────
9: 
10: export type IncomingMessageType =
11: 	| "message"
12: 	| "permission_response"
13: 	| "ask_user_response"
14: 	| "question_reject"
15: 	| "new_session"
16: 	| "switch_session"
17: 	| "delete_session"
18: 	| "rename_session"
19: 	| "list_sessions"
20: 	| "search_sessions"
21: 	| "load_more_history"
22: 	| "terminal_command"
23: 	| "input_sync"
24: 	| "switch_agent"
25: 	| "switch_model"
26: 	| "get_todo"
27: 	| "get_agents"
28: 	| "get_models"
29: 	| "get_commands"
30: 	| "get_projects"
31: 	| "add_project"
32: 	| "get_file_list"
33: 	| "get_file_content"
34: 	| "pty_create"
35: 	| "pty_input"
36: 	| "pty_resize"
37: 	| "pty_close"
38: 	| "cancel"
39: 	| "rewind";
40: 
41: const VALID_MESSAGE_TYPES = new Set<string>([
42: 	"message",
43: 	"permission_response",
44: 	"ask_user_response",
45: 	"question_reject",
46: 	"new_session",
47: 	"switch_session",
48: 	"delete_session",
49: 	"rename_session",
50: 	"list_sessions",
51: 	"search_sessions",
52: 	"load_more_history",
53: 	"terminal_command",
54: 	"input_sync",
55: 	"switch_agent",
56: 	"switch_model",
57: 	"get_todo",
58: 	"get_agents",
59: 	"get_models",
60: 	"get_commands",
61: 	"get_projects",
62: 	"add_project",
63: 	"get_file_list",
64: 	"get_file_content",
65: 	"pty_create",
66: 	"pty_input",
67: 	"pty_resize",
68: 	"pty_close",
69: 	"cancel",
70: 	"rewind",
71: ]);
72: 
73: export interface IncomingMessage {
74: 	type: string;
75: 	[key: string]: unknown;
76: }
77: 
78: export interface RouteResult {
79: 	handler: IncomingMessageType;
80: 	payload: Record<string, unknown>;
81: }
82: 
83: export interface ErrorResult {
84: 	type: "error";
85: 	code: string;
86: 	message: string;
87: }
88: 
89: // ─── Message routing ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
90: 
91: /**
92:  * Parse and validate an incoming WebSocket message.
93:  * Returns the parsed object or null if parsing fails.
94:  */
95: export function parseIncomingMessage(raw: string): IncomingMessage | null {
96: 	try {
97: 		const parsed = JSON.parse(raw);
98: 		if (
99: 			typeof parsed !== "object" ||
100: 			parsed === null ||
101: 			Array.isArray(parsed)
102: 		) {
103: 			return null;
104: 		}
105: 		if (typeof parsed.type !== "string") {
106: 			return null;
107: 		}
108: 		return parsed as IncomingMessage;
109: 	} catch {
110: 		return null;
111: 	}
112: }
113: 
114: /**
115:  * Route a parsed message to its handler.
116:  * Returns either a RouteResult or an ErrorResult for unknown types.
117:  */
118: export function routeMessage(msg: IncomingMessage): RouteResult | ErrorResult {
119: 	if (!VALID_MESSAGE_TYPES.has(msg.type)) {
120: 		return {
121: 			type: "error",
122: 			code: "UNKNOWN_MESSAGE_TYPE",
123: 			message: `Unknown message type: ${msg.type}`,
124: 		};
125: 	}
126: 
127: 	const { type, ...payload } = msg;
128: 	return {
129: 		handler: type as IncomingMessageType,
130: 		payload,
131: 	};
132: }
133: 
134: /**
135:  * Check if a route result is an error.
136:  */
137: export function isRouteError(
138: 	result: RouteResult | ErrorResult,
139: ): result is ErrorResult {
140: 	return "type" in result && (result as ErrorResult).type === "error";
141: }
142: 
143: // ─── Client tracking ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
144: 
145: export interface ClientTracker {
146: 	addClient(clientId: string): number;
147: 	removeClient(clientId: string): number;
148: 	getClientCount(): number;
149: 	getClientIds(): string[];
150: 	hasClient(clientId: string): boolean;
151: 	getBroadcastTargets(excludeClientId?: string): string[];
152: }
153: 
154: /**
155:  * Create a client tracker for managing connected WebSocket clients.
156:  */
157: export function createClientTracker(): ClientTracker {
158: 	const clients = new Set<string>();
159: 
160: 	return {
161: 		addClient(clientId: string): number {
162: 			clients.add(clientId);
163: 			return clients.size;
164: 		},
165: 
166: 		removeClient(clientId: string): number {
167: 			clients.delete(clientId);
168: 			return clients.size;
169: 		},
170: 
171: 		getClientCount(): number {
172: 			return clients.size;
173: 		},
174: 
175: 		getClientIds(): string[] {
176: 			return [...clients];
177: 		},
178: 
179: 		hasClient(clientId: string): boolean {
180: 			return clients.has(clientId);
181: 		},
182: 
183: 		getBroadcastTargets(excludeClientId?: string): string[] {
184: 			if (!excludeClientId) return [...clients];
185: 			return [...clients].filter((id) => id !== excludeClientId);
186: 		},
187: 	};
188: }
189: 
190: // ─── State snapshot ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
191: 
192: export interface SessionState {
193: 	sessionId: string;
194: 	title: string;
195: 	model?: string;
196: 	provider?: string;
197: 	status: "idle" | "processing";
198: 	clientCount: number;
199: }
200: 
201: export interface PendingState {
202: 	permissions: Array<{ requestId: string; toolName: string }>;
203: 	questions: Array<{ toolId: string; questions: unknown[] }>;
204: }
205: 
206: export interface StateSnapshot {
207: 	session: SessionState;
208: 	pending: PendingState;
209: 	clientCount: number;
210: }
211: 
212: /**
213:  * Build a state snapshot for a newly connected client.
214:  * The snapshot includes the session state, pending interactions, and client count.
215:  */
216: export function buildStateSnapshot(
217: 	session: SessionState,
218: 	pending: PendingState,
219: 	clientCount: number,
220: ): StateSnapshot {
221: 	return {
222: 		session: { ...session, clientCount },
223: 		pending: {
224: 			permissions: [...pending.permissions],
225: 			questions: [...pending.questions],
226: 		},
227: 		clientCount,
228: 	};
229: }
230: 
231: /**
232:  * Build the list of messages to send to a newly connected client.
233:  * Returns RelayMessage[] in the correct order per AC5.
234:  */
235: export function buildNewClientMessages(
236: 	snapshot: StateSnapshot,
237: ): RelayMessage[] {
238: 	const messages: RelayMessage[] = [];
239: 
240: 	// 1. Status
241: 	messages.push({
242: 		type: "status",
243: 		status: snapshot.session.status,
244: 	});
245: 
246: 	// 2. Client count
247: 	messages.push({
248: 		type: "client_count",
249: 		count: snapshot.clientCount,
250: 	});
251: 
252: 	// 3. Pending permissions
253: 	for (const perm of snapshot.pending.permissions) {
254: 		messages.push({
255: 			type: "permission_request",
256: 			requestId: perm.requestId,
257: 			toolName: perm.toolName,
258: 			toolInput: {},
259: 		});
260: 	}
261: 
262: 	// 4. Pending questions
263: 	for (const q of snapshot.pending.questions) {
264: 		messages.push({
265: 			type: "ask_user",
266: 			toolId: q.toolId,
267: 			questions: q.questions as RelayMessage extends { type: "ask_user" }
268: 				? never
269: 				: unknown[],
270: 		} as RelayMessage);
271: 	}
272: 
273: 	return messages;
274: }
275: 
276: // ─── Client count message factory ────────────────────────────────────────────
277: 
278: export function createClientCountMessage(count: number): RelayMessage {
279: 	return { type: "client_count", count };
280: }

(End of file - total 280 lines)
</content>

<path>/Users/dstern/src/personal/opencode-relay/opencode-relay/.worktrees/ticket-6.2/src/lib/relay-stack.ts</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: // ─── Relay Stack ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // The complete relay wiring: OpenCode client, SSE consumer, event translator,
3: // WebSocket handler, session manager, permission/question bridges.
4: //
5: // Extracted from skeleton.ts so integration tests exercise the exact same
6: // wiring as production. skeleton.ts is now a thin CLI wrapper around this.
7: 
8: import { stat } from "node:fs/promises";
9: import { createRequire } from "node:module";
10: import { homedir } from "node:os";
11: import { join, resolve } from "node:path";
12: import { type ClientInitDeps, handleClientConnected } from "./client-init.js";
13: import { formatErrorDetail, RelayError } from "./errors.js";
14: import { createTranslator } from "./event-translator.js";
15: import { dispatchMessage, type HandlerDeps } from "./handlers/index.js";
16: import { getClientIp, parseCookies } from "./http-utils.js";
17: import { generateSlug } from "./ipc-protocol.js";
18: import { MessageCache } from "./message-cache.js";
19: import { OpenCodeClient } from "./opencode-client.js";
20: import { PermissionBridge } from "./permission-bridge.js";
21: import { PtyManager, type PtyUpstream } from "./pty-manager.js";
22: import type { PushNotificationManager } from "./push.js";
23: import { QuestionBridge } from "./question-bridge.js";
24: import { RelayServer } from "./server.js";
25: import { SessionManager } from "./session-manager.js";
26: import { SessionOverrides } from "./session-overrides.js";
27: import { SSEConsumer } from "./sse-consumer.js";
28: import { wireSSEConsumer } from "./sse-wiring.js";
29: import type { PartType, ProjectRelayConfig, ToolStatus } from "./types.js";
30: import { WebSocketHandler } from "./ws-handler.js";
31: 
32: // ─── WebSocket library for upstream PTY connections ─────────────────────────
33: const requireWs = createRequire(import.meta.url);
34: const wsLib = requireWs("ws");
35: const WebSocketClass = wsLib.WebSocket as typeof import("ws").WebSocket;
36: type RawData = import("ws").RawData;
37: 
38: /** Per-project relay: all relay components attached to a shared server. */
39: export interface ProjectRelay {
40: 	wsHandler: WebSocketHandler;
41: 	sseConsumer: SSEConsumer;
42: 	client: OpenCodeClient;
43: 	sessionMgr: SessionManager;
44: 	translator: ReturnType<typeof createTranslator>;
45: 	permissionBridge: PermissionBridge;
46: 	questionBridge: QuestionBridge;
47: 	/** Gracefully stop relay components (SSE + WebSocket). Does NOT stop the HTTP server. */
48: 	stop(): Promise<void>;
49: }
50: 
51: // ─── Full Stack Config ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
52: 
53: export interface RelayStackConfig {
54: 	port: number;
55: 	host?: string;
56: 	opencodeUrl: string;
57: 	pin?: string;
58: 	projectDir: string;
59: 	slug: string;
60: 	staticDir?: string;
61: 	/** TLS certificate and key for HTTPS mode */
62: 	tls?: { key: Buffer; cert: Buffer; caRoot?: string };
63: 	/** Session title for the initial session */
64: 	sessionTitle?: string;
65: 	/** Log function — defaults to console.log, pass () => {} to silence */
66: 	log?: (...args: unknown[]) => void;
67: 	/** Optional pre-initialized push notification manager */
68: 	pushManager?: PushNotificationManager;
69: }
70: 
71: // ─── Stack ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
72: 
73: export interface RelayStack {
74: 	server: RelayServer;
75: 	wsHandler: WebSocketHandler;
76: 	sseConsumer: SSEConsumer;
77: 	client: OpenCodeClient;
78: 	sessionMgr: SessionManager;
79: 	translator: ReturnType<typeof createTranslator>;
80: 	permissionBridge: PermissionBridge;
81: 	questionBridge: QuestionBridge;
82: 
83: 	/** The port the HTTP server is actually listening on (useful when port=0) */
84: 	getPort(): number;
85: 	/** The base URL of the relay server */
86: 	getBaseUrl(): string;
87: 	/** Stop all components */
88: 	stop(): Promise<void>;
89: }
90: 
91: // ─── Create Per-Project Relay ────────────────────────────────────────────────
92: 
93: /**
94:  * Create a per-project relay that attaches to an existing HTTP server.
95:  *
96:  * Sets up all relay components (OpenCode client, SSE consumer, translator,
97:  * session manager, bridges, WebSocket handler) and wires the full event
98:  * pipeline. Does NOT create or manage an HTTP server — the caller owns it.
99:  *
100:  * Used by both `createRelayStack()` (for standalone/skeleton mode) and the
101:  * daemon (which has its own HTTP server).
102:  */
103: export async function createProjectRelay(
104: 	config: ProjectRelayConfig,
105: ): Promise<ProjectRelay> {
106: 	const log = config.log ?? console.log;
107: 
108: 	// ── Components ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
109: 
110: 	const client = new OpenCodeClient({
111: 		baseUrl: config.opencodeUrl,
112: 		directory: config.noServer ? config.projectDir : undefined,
113: 	});
114: 	const translator = createTranslator();
115: 	const permissionBridge = new PermissionBridge();
116: 	const questionBridge = new QuestionBridge();
117: 	const sessionMgr = new SessionManager({ client });
118: 
119: 	// ── Per-session event cache (identical to claude-relay's doSendAndRecord) ──
120: 	const cacheDir = join(
121: 		config.projectDir ?? process.cwd(),
122: 		".opencode-relay",
123: 		"sessions",
124: 	);
125: 	const messageCache = new MessageCache(cacheDir);
126: 	messageCache.loadFromDisk();
127: 
128: 	// Per-session overrides (agent, model, processing timeout)
129: 	const overrides = new SessionOverrides();
130: 
131: 	// ── PTY sessions with server-side scrollback (claude-relay architecture) ──
132: 	// Each active PTY gets one upstream WebSocket to OpenCode's /pty/:id/connect.
133: 	// Output is buffered server-side (50 KB FIFO per terminal) and broadcast to
134: 	// ALL browser clients. New clients get scrollback replayed on connect.
135: 	// Input from any browser client is forwarded to the shared upstream WS.
136: 	// PTYs persist across browser show/hide toggles — only closed on explicit
137: 	// pty_close (tab X button) or upstream disconnect.
138: 
139: 	const ptyManager = new PtyManager({ log });
140: 
141: 	async function connectPtyUpstream(
142: 		ptyId: string,
143: 		cursor: number = 0,
144: 	): Promise<void> {
145: 		// cursor=0 : replay entire PTY buffer (use for newly-created PTYs so the
146: 		//            initial shell prompt is delivered).
147: 		// cursor=-1: skip buffered data (use when reconnecting to an existing PTY
148: 		//            to avoid replaying stale escape sequences).
149: 		const httpUrl = new URL(
150: 			`/pty/${ptyId}/connect?cursor=${cursor}`,
151: 			config.opencodeUrl,
152: 		);
153: 		const wsUrl = httpUrl.toString().replace(/^http/, "ws");
154: 		const headers = client.getAuthHeaders();
155: 
156: 		return new Promise<void>((resolve, reject) => {
157: 			const upstream = new WebSocketClass(wsUrl, {
158: 				headers,
159: 				// Disable per-message compression for PTY connections.
160: 				// Bun's WS may negotiate permessage-deflate with Node's ws, and
161: 				// decompression bugs or flush mismatches can corrupt binary PTY data.
162: 				perMessageDeflate: false,
163: 				// Skip UTF-8 validation for TEXT frames. Terminal data can contain
164: 				// 8-bit characters (C1 control codes) that are valid in terminal
165: 				// protocols but technically invalid in strict UTF-8. Without this,
166: 				// the ws library would close the connection on such frames.
167: 				skipUTF8Validation: true,
168: 			});
169: 
170: 			// Register session BEFORE any events can fire. This mirrors the
171: 			// original pattern where the PtySession object was created before
172: 			// the WebSocket — prevents the race condition where OpenCode sends
173: 			// replay data in the same TCP segment as the upgrade response
174: 			// (Node.js ws processes both synchronously).
175: 			ptyManager.registerSession(ptyId, upstream as unknown as PtyUpstream);
176: 			let connected = false;
177: 
178: 			const connectTimeout = setTimeout(() => {
179: 				// Remove the session on timeout — it was never successfully opened
180: 				ptyManager.closeSession(ptyId);
181: 				reject(new Error(`Timed out connecting to PTY ${ptyId}`));
182: 			}, 10_000);
183: 
184: 			// Install message handler BEFORE "open" fires.
185: 			upstream.on("message", (data: RawData) => {
186: 				// OpenCode sends cursor metadata as binary: 0x00 + JSON {"cursor":N}.
187: 				// Always convert to Buffer first and check byte 0.
188: 				let buf: Buffer;
189: 				if (Buffer.isBuffer(data)) {
190: 					buf = data;
191: 				} else if (typeof data === "string") {
192: 					buf = Buffer.from(data);
193: 				} else {
194: 					buf = Buffer.from(data as ArrayBuffer);
195: 				}
196: 
197: 				if (buf.length > 0 && buf[0] === 0x00) return;
198: 				const text = buf.toString();
199: 				if (!text) return;
200: 
201: 				// Buffer scrollback via PtyManager (FIFO, 50 KB cap)
202: 				ptyManager.appendScrollback(ptyId, text);
203: 
204: 				// Broadcast to all browser clients
205: 				wsHandler.broadcast({ type: "pty_output", ptyId, data: text });
206: 			});
207: 
208: 			upstream.on("close", () => {
209: 				ptyManager.markExited(ptyId, 0);
210: 				// Only broadcast pty_exited if the session is still tracked.
211: 				// closeSession deletes + broadcasts pty_deleted, so we must
212: 				// not also broadcast pty_exited for the same PTY.  Similarly,
213: 				// if connectPtyUpstream failed the session was never stored.
214: 				if (ptyManager.hasSession(ptyId)) {
215: 					wsHandler.broadcast({
216: 						type: "pty_exited",
217: 						ptyId,
218: 						exitCode: 0,
219: 					});
220: 				}
221: 				log(`   [pty] Upstream closed: ${ptyId}`);
222: 			});
223: 
224: 			upstream.on("error", (err: Error) => {
225: 				if (!connected) {
226: 					clearTimeout(connectTimeout);
227: 					// Remove the failed session
228: 					ptyManager.closeSession(ptyId);
229: 					reject(err);
230: 				}
231: 				log(`   [pty] Upstream error ${ptyId}: ${err.message}`);
232: 			});
233: 
234: 			upstream.on("open", () => {
235: 				connected = true;
236: 				clearTimeout(connectTimeout);
237: 				resolve();
238: 			});
239: 		});
240: 	}
241: 
242: 	// ── Health check ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
243: 
244: 	await client.getHealth();
245: 	log(`   ✓ OpenCode is reachable at ${config.opencodeUrl}`);
246: 
247: 	// ── Session ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
248: 
249: 	const sessionId = await sessionMgr.initialize(
250: 		config.sessionTitle ?? "Relay Session",
251: 	);
252: 	log(`   ✓ Using session: ${sessionId}`);
253: 
254: 	// ── WebSocket handler ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
255: 
256: 	const wsHandler = new WebSocketHandler(
257: 		config.noServer ? null : config.httpServer,
258: 		{
259: 			// In noServer mode, the daemon's upgrade handler checks auth before
260: 			// calling handleUpgrade(). Skip verifyClient to avoid double auth.
261: 			verifyClient: config.noServer ? undefined : config.verifyClient,
262: 		},
263: 	);
264: 
265: 	const clientInitDeps: ClientInitDeps = {
266: 		wsHandler,
267: 		client,
268: 		sessionMgr,
269: 		messageCache,
270: 		overrides,
271: 		ptyManager,
272: 		permissionBridge,
273: 		questionBridge,
274: 		log,
275: 	};
276: 
277: 	wsHandler.on("client_connected", ({ clientId }) => {
278: 		log(`   [ws] Client connected: ${clientId}`);
279: 		handleClientConnected(clientInitDeps, clientId).catch((err) =>
280: 			log(
281: 				`   [ws] Client init failed for ${clientId}: ${formatErrorDetail(err)}`,
282: 			),
283: 		);
284: 	});
285: 
286: 	wsHandler.on("client_disconnected", ({ clientId }) => {
287: 		log(`   [ws] Client disconnected: ${clientId}`);
288: 	});
289: 
290: 	// ── SSE consumer ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
291: 
292: 	const sseConsumer = new SSEConsumer({
293: 		baseUrl: config.opencodeUrl,
294: 		authHeaders: client.getAuthHeaders(),
295: 	});
296: 
297: 	// ── Wire session manager → WebSocket ────────────────────────────────────
298: 
299: 	sessionMgr.on("broadcast", (msg) => {
300: 		// Augment session_switched with cached events (combined protocol)
301: 		if (msg.type === "session_switched") {
302: 			const switchId = (msg as { id?: string }).id;
303: 			if (switchId) {
304: 				const events = messageCache.getEvents(switchId);
305: 				const hasChatContent =
306: 					events?.some(
307: 						(e) => e.type === "user_message" || e.type === "delta",
308: 					) ?? false;
309: 				if (events && hasChatContent) {
310: 					wsHandler.broadcast({ ...msg, events });
311: 					return;
312: 				}
313: 			}
314: 		}
315: 		wsHandler.broadcast(msg);
316: 	});
317: 	sessionMgr.on("session_changed", async ({ sessionId: sid }) => {
318: 		translator.reset();
319: 		// Rebuild translator state from REST API so it knows which parts already
320: 		// exist in the session we're switching to (prevents duplicate tool_start).
321: 		try {
322: 			const messages = await client.getMessages(sid);
323: 			const parts: Array<{
324: 				parts?: Array<{
325: 					id: string;
326: 					type: PartType;
327: 					state?: { status?: ToolStatus };
328: 				}>;
329: 			}> = messages.map((m) => ({
330: 				parts: (m as { parts?: unknown[] }).parts as
331: 					| Array<{
332: 							id: string;
333: 							type: PartType;
334: 							state?: { status?: ToolStatus };
335: 					  }>
336: 					| undefined,
337: 			}));
338: 			translator.rebuildStateFromHistory(parts);
339: 		} catch {
340: 			// Non-fatal — translator will treat all parts as new
341: 		}
342: 	});
343: 
344: 	// ── Handle incoming messages from browser ───────────────────────────────
345: 	// Messages are serialized: each handler completes before the next starts.
346: 	// Without this, concurrent async handlers (e.g. new_session + switch_session)
347: 	// race — createSession's broadcast can arrive on the client AFTER
348: 	// a switch_session reply, clearing messages the user just switched to.
349: 
350: 	const handlerDeps: HandlerDeps = {
351: 		wsHandler,
352: 		client,
353: 		sessionMgr,
354: 		messageCache,
355: 		permissionBridge,
356: 		questionBridge,
357: 		overrides,
358: 		ptyManager,
359: 		config,
360: 		log,
361: 		connectPtyUpstream,
362: 	};
363: 
364: 	let _messageQueue = Promise.resolve();
365: 
366: 	wsHandler.on("message", ({ clientId, handler, payload }) => {
367: 		_messageQueue = _messageQueue.then(async () => {
368: 			try {
369: 				await dispatchMessage(handlerDeps, clientId, handler, payload);
370: 			} catch (err) {
371: 				log(`   [ws] Error handling ${handler}:`, formatErrorDetail(err));
372: 				wsHandler.sendTo(
373: 					clientId,
374: 					RelayError.fromCaught(err, "HANDLER_ERROR").toMessage(),
375: 				);
376: 			}
377: 		});
378: 	});
379: 
380: 	// ── SSE event wiring (translate → filter → cache → broadcast) ──────────
381: 
382: 	wireSSEConsumer(
383: 		{
384: 			translator,
385: 			sessionMgr,
386: 			messageCache,
387: 			permissionBridge,
388: 			questionBridge,
389: 			overrides,
390: 			wsHandler,
391: 			pushManager: config.pushManager,
392: 			log,
393: 		},
394: 		sseConsumer,
395: 	);
396: 
397: 	await sseConsumer.connect();
398: 
399: 	// ── Permission/question timeout checks ──────────────────────────────────
400: 
401: 	const timeoutTimer = setInterval(() => {
402: 		const timedOutPerms = permissionBridge.checkTimeouts();
403: 		for (const id of timedOutPerms) {
404: 			wsHandler.broadcast({
405: 				type: "permission_resolved",
406: 				requestId: id,
407: 				decision: "timeout",
408: 			});
409: 		}
410: 		const timedOutQuestions = questionBridge.checkTimeouts();
411: 		for (const id of timedOutQuestions) {
412: 			questionBridge.remove(id);
413: 			wsHandler.broadcast({ type: "ask_user_resolved", toolId: id });
414: 		}
415: 	}, 30_000);
416: 
417: 	// Don't let the timer keep the process alive
418: 	if (
419: 		timeoutTimer &&
420: 		typeof timeoutTimer === "object" &&
421: 		"unref" in timeoutTimer
422: 	) {
423: 		timeoutTimer.unref();
424: 	}
425: 
426: 	// ── Return project relay ────────────────────────────────────────────────
427: 
428: 	return {
429: 		wsHandler,
430: 		sseConsumer,
431: 		client,
432: 		sessionMgr,
433: 		translator,
434: 		permissionBridge,
435: 		questionBridge,
436: 
437: 		async stop() {
438: 			clearInterval(timeoutTimer);
439: 			overrides.dispose();
440: 			ptyManager.closeAll();
441: 			await sseConsumer.disconnect();
442: 			wsHandler.close();
443: 		},
444: 	};
445: }
446: 
447: // ─── Create Full Stack (Server + Relay) ─────────────────────────────────────
448: 
449: /**
450:  * Create a full relay stack with its own HTTP server.
451:  *
452:  * Creates a RelayServer, registers the project, starts the server, then
453:  * delegates to `createProjectRelay()` for all relay wiring. Used by
454:  * skeleton.ts for standalone operation.
455:  */
456: export async function createRelayStack(
457: 	config: RelayStackConfig,
458: ): Promise<RelayStack> {
459: 	const log = config.log ?? console.log;
460: 
461: 	// ── Push notification manager ────────────────────────────────────────────
462: 
463: 	let pushMgr: PushNotificationManager | undefined = config.pushManager;
464: 	if (!pushMgr) {
465: 		try {
466: 			const { PushNotificationManager } = await import("./push.js");
467: 			pushMgr = new PushNotificationManager();
468: 			await pushMgr.init();
469: 		} catch {
470: 			pushMgr = undefined;
471: 		}
472: 	}
473: 
474: 	// ── HTTP server ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
475: 
476: 	const server = new RelayServer({
477: 		port: config.port,
478: 		host: config.host,
479: 		pin: config.pin || undefined,
480: 		staticDir: config.staticDir,
481: 		tls: config.tls,
482: 		pushManager: pushMgr,
483: 	});
484: 
485: 	server.addProject({
486: 		slug: config.slug,
487: 		directory: config.projectDir,
488: 		title: config.slug,
489: 	});
490: 
491: 	await server.start();
492: 
493: 	const maybeServer = server.getHttpServer();
494: 	if (!maybeServer) {
495: 		throw new Error("HTTP server not available after start()");
496: 	}
497: 	// Assign to a fresh const so TypeScript narrows to non-null in closures.
498: 	const httpServer = maybeServer;
499: 
500: 	// ── Multi-project relay management ──────────────────────────────────────
501: 	// All relays use noServer mode. A single upgrade handler routes WebSocket
502: 	// connections to the correct relay by URL path (/ws → initial, /p/{slug}/ws → project).
503: 	// This matches the daemon pattern and allows dynamic project addition.
504: 
505: 	const relays = new Map<string, ProjectRelay>();
506: 	const pendingSlugs = new Set<string>();
507: 
508: 	const getProjectList = () =>
509: 		server.getProjects().map((p) => ({
510: 			slug: p.slug,
511: 			title: p.title,
512: 			directory: p.directory,
513: 		}));
514: 
515: 	/** Create a new project relay and register it. */
516: 	async function addProjectRelay(
517: 		directory: string,
518: 	): Promise<{ slug: string; title: string; directory: string }> {
519: 		// Expand ~ and resolve to absolute path
520: 		if (directory.startsWith("~/") || directory === "~") {
521: 			directory = directory.replace("~", homedir());
522: 		}
523: 		directory = resolve(directory);
524: 
525: 		// Check if directory is already registered
526: 		for (const p of server.getProjects()) {
527: 			if (p.directory === directory) {
528: 				return { slug: p.slug, title: p.title, directory: p.directory };
529: 			}
530: 		}
531: 
532: 		// Validate directory exists on disk
533: 		const dirStat = await stat(directory).catch(() => null);
534: 		if (!dirStat?.isDirectory()) {
535: 			throw new Error(
536: 				dirStat
537: 					? `Not a directory: ${directory}`
538: 					: `Directory does not exist: ${directory}`,
539: 			);
540: 		}
541: 
542: 		const existingSlugs = new Set(relays.keys());
543: 		const slug = generateSlug(directory, existingSlugs);
544: 		const parts = directory.replace(/\\/g, "/").split("/").filter(Boolean);
545: 		const title = parts[parts.length - 1] ?? "project";
546: 
547: 		// Guard against concurrent creation for the same slug
548: 		if (relays.has(slug) || pendingSlugs.has(slug)) {
549: 			const existing = relays.get(slug);
550: 			if (existing) return { slug, title, directory };
551: 			throw new Error(`Relay for ${directory} is still being created`);
552: 		}
553: 
554: 		pendingSlugs.add(slug);
555: 		try {
556: 			// Create relay FIRST — if this throws, nothing is registered
557: 			const newRelay = await createProjectRelay({
558: 				httpServer,
559: 				opencodeUrl: config.opencodeUrl,
560: 				projectDir: directory,
561: 				slug,
562: 				noServer: true,
563: 				sessionTitle: config.sessionTitle,
564: 				log,
565: 				getProjects: getProjectList,
566: 				addProject: addProjectRelay,
567: 				pushManager: pushMgr,
568: 			});
569: 
570: 			// Only register AFTER relay is successfully created
571: 			relays.set(slug, newRelay);
572: 			server.addProject({ slug, directory, title });
573: 
574: 			log(`   [project] Added: ${title} (${slug}) → ${directory}`);
575: 		} catch (err) {
576: 			// Clean up on failure — no zombie entries
577: 			relays.delete(slug);
578: 			log(`   [project] Failed to add ${directory}: ${formatErrorDetail(err)}`);
579: 			throw err;
580: 		} finally {
581: 			pendingSlugs.delete(slug);
582: 		}
583: 
584: 		return { slug, title, directory };
585: 	}
586: 
587: 	// ── Initial project relay ───────────────────────────────────────────────
588: 
589: 	const relay = await createProjectRelay({
590: 		httpServer,
591: 		opencodeUrl: config.opencodeUrl,
592: 		projectDir: config.projectDir,
593: 		slug: config.slug,
594: 		sessionTitle: config.sessionTitle,
595: 		log,
596: 		noServer: true,
597: 		getProjects: getProjectList,
598: 		addProject: addProjectRelay,
599: 		pushManager: pushMgr,
600: 	});
601: 	relays.set(config.slug, relay);
602: 
603: 	// ── WebSocket upgrade handler ───────────────────────────────────────────
604: 	// Routes connections by URL: /p/{slug}/ws → project relay, /ws → initial relay.
605: 	// Also checks auth when a PIN is configured (fixes pre-existing gap where
606: 	// standalone WS connections bypassed PIN auth).
607: 
608: 	httpServer.on("upgrade", (req, socket, head) => {
609: 		// Auth check (mirrors server.ts private checkAuth)
610: 		const auth = server.getAuth();
611: 		if (auth.hasPin()) {
612: 			const cookies = parseCookies(req.headers.cookie ?? "");
613: 			const sessionCookie = cookies.relay_session;
614: 			const cookieOk = sessionCookie
615: 				? auth.validateCookie(sessionCookie)
616: 				: false;
617: 			if (!cookieOk) {
618: 				const pinHeader = req.headers["x-relay-pin"];
619: 				const pinOk =
620: 					typeof pinHeader === "string" &&
621: 					auth.authenticate(pinHeader, getClientIp(req)).ok;
622: 				if (!pinOk) {
623: 					socket.destroy();
624: 					return;
625: 				}
626: 			}
627: 		}
628: 
629: 		// Route /p/{slug}/ws → project relay
630: 		const projectMatch = req.url?.match(/^\/p\/([^/]+)\/ws(?:\?|$)/);
631: 		if (projectMatch) {
632: 			const target = relays.get(projectMatch[1]!);
633: 			if (target) {
634: 				target.wsHandler.handleUpgrade(req, socket, head);
635: 			} else {
636: 				socket.destroy();
637: 			}
638: 			return;
639: 		}
640: 
641: 		// Route /ws → initial relay
642: 		if (req.url === "/ws" || req.url?.startsWith("/ws?")) {
643: 			relay.wsHandler.handleUpgrade(req, socket, head);
644: 			return;
645: 		}
646: 
647: 		socket.destroy();
648: 	});
649: 
650: 	const urls = server.getUrls();
651: 	log(`   ✓ Server listening: ${urls.local}`);
652: 
653: 	return {
654: 		server,
655: 		wsHandler: relay.wsHandler,
656: 		sseConsumer: relay.sseConsumer,
657: 		client: relay.client,
658: 		sessionMgr: relay.sessionMgr,
659: 		translator: relay.translator,
660: 		permissionBridge: relay.permissionBridge,
661: 		questionBridge: relay.questionBridge,
662: 
663: 		getPort() {
664: 			const addr = httpServer.address();
665: 			if (typeof addr === "object" && addr) return addr.port;
666: 			return config.port;
667: 		},
668: 
669: 		getBaseUrl() {
670: 			const addr = httpServer.address();
671: 			const port = typeof addr === "object" && addr ? addr.port : config.port;
672: 			const protocol = config.tls ? "https" : "http";
673: 			return `${protocol}://127.0.0.1:${port}`;
674: 		},
675: 
676: 		async stop() {
677: 			for (const r of relays.values()) {
678: 				try {
679: 					await r.stop();
680: 				} catch {
681: 					/* ignore */
682: 				}
683: 			}
684: 			relays.clear();
685: 			await server.stop();
686: 		},
687: 	};
688: }

388: **Step 3: Implement truncation utility**
389: 
390: ```typescript
391: // src/lib/truncate-content.ts
392: import type { RelayMessage } from "./types.js";
393: 
394: export const TRUNCATION_THRESHOLD = 50_000;
395: 
396: export interface TruncationResult {
397: 	content: string;
398: 	isTruncated: boolean;
399: 	fullContentLength?: number;
400: }
401: 
402: export function truncateContent(content: string, threshold = TRUNCATION_THRESHOLD): TruncationResult {
403: 	if (content.length <= threshold) return { content, isTruncated: false };
404: 	return { content: content.slice(0, threshold), isTruncated: true, fullContentLength: content.length };
405: }
406: 
407: type ToolResultMessage = Extract<RelayMessage, { type: "tool_result" }>;
408: 
409: export interface ToolResultTruncation {
410: 	truncated: ToolResultMessage;
411: 	fullContent: string | undefined;
412: }
413: 
414: export function truncateToolResult(msg: ToolResultMessage, threshold = TRUNCATION_THRESHOLD): ToolResultTruncation {
415: 	const result = truncateContent(msg.content, threshold);
416: 	if (!result.isTruncated) return { truncated: msg, fullContent: undefined };
417: 	return {
418: 		truncated: { ...msg, content: result.content, isTruncated: true, fullContentLength: result.fullContentLength },
419: 		fullContent: msg.content,
420: 	};
421: }
422: ```

<content>1: # Ticket 6.2 — Remaining Error Handling & Edge Cases
2: 
3: > **For Claude:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task.
4: 
5: **Goal:** Complete all remaining acceptance criteria from ticket 6.2: OpenCode connection lifecycle (AC1/AC2), browser reconnection (AC3), large tool result truncation (AC5), rate limiting (AC7), and disk space monitoring (AC8). AC4 and AC6 are already implemented.
6: 
7: **Architecture:** AC1/AC2 wires the existing `connection_status` RelayMessage variant into the SSE wiring layer, broadcasting to browsers when OpenCode connects/disconnects, with frontend banner handling. AC3 wires the existing dead-code `buildNewClientMessages()` into `handleClientConnected()` so reconnecting browsers receive pending permissions/questions. AC5 truncates tool results >50KB at the relay-stack layer, stores full content in an in-memory `ToolContentStore`, and adds a "Show full output" button. AC7 adds a sliding-window rate limiter (5 messages/10s) on both client and server. AC8 creates a `StorageMonitor` wired into the Daemon lifecycle with cache eviction on low disk.
8: 
9: **Tech Stack:** TypeScript, Svelte 5, Vitest, Node.js `fs.statfs()`
10: 
11: **Status of existing implementation:**
12: - `connection_status` type defined in `shared-types.ts` but never sent or handled
13: - `buildNewClientMessages()` exists in `ws-router.ts:235-274` but never called
14: - SSE consumer has reconnect logic but never broadcasts status to browsers
15: - AC4 (permission dedup) and AC6 (invalid WS messages) are fully implemented
16: 
17: ---
18: 
19: ## AC1/AC2 — OpenCode Connection Lifecycle
20: 
21: ### Task 1: Broadcast connection_status from SSE wiring + frontend banner
22: 
23: **Files:**
24: - Modify: `src/lib/sse-wiring.ts` (~line 213-243 — SSE event handlers for connected/disconnected/reconnecting)

250: // src/lib/tool-content-store.ts
251: 
252: /** In-memory store for full tool result content (before truncation). */
253: export interface ToolContentStoreOptions {
254: 	/** Max entries before evicting oldest. Default: 500. */
255: 	maxEntries?: number;
256: }
257: 
258: interface Entry {
259: 	content: string;
260: 	sessionId?: string;
261: 	storedAt: number;
262: }
263: 
264: export class ToolContentStore {
265: 	private readonly entries = new Map<string, Entry>();
266: 	private readonly maxEntries: number;
267: 
268: 	constructor(options?: ToolContentStoreOptions) {
269: 		this.maxEntries = options?.maxEntries ?? 500;
270: 	}
271: 
272: 	store(toolId: string, content: string, sessionId?: string): void {
273: 		this.entries.set(toolId, { content, sessionId, storedAt: Date.now() });
274: 		this.evictIfNeeded();
275: 	}
276: 
277: 	get(toolId: string): string | undefined {
278: 		return this.entries.get(toolId)?.content;
279: 	}
280: 
281: 	clearSession(sessionId: string): void {
282: 		for (const [id, entry] of this.entries) {
283: 			if (entry.sessionId === sessionId) {
284: 				this.entries.delete(id);
285: 			}
286: 		}
287: 	}
288: 
289: 	get size(): number {
290: 		return this.entries.size;
291: 	}
292: 
293: 	private evictIfNeeded(): void {
294: 		while (this.entries.size > this.maxEntries) {
295: 			const oldest = this.entries.keys().next().value;
296: 			if (oldest !== undefined) this.entries.delete(oldest);
297: 		}
298: 	}
299: }
300: ```

<content>1: # Implementer Subagent Prompt Template
2: 
3: Use this template when dispatching an implementer subagent.
4: 
5: ```
6: Task tool (general-purpose):
7:   description: "Implement Task N: [task name]"
8:   prompt: |
9:     You are implementing Task N: [task name]
10: 
11:     ## Task Description
12: 
13:     [FULL TEXT of task from plan - paste it here, don't make subagent read file]
14: 
15:     ## Context
16: 
17:     [Scene-setting: where this fits, dependencies, architectural context]
18: 
19:     ## Before You Begin
20: 
21:     If you have questions about:
22:     - The requirements or acceptance criteria
23:     - The approach or implementation strategy
24:     - Dependencies or assumptions
25:     - Anything unclear in the task description
26: 
27:     **Ask them now.** Raise any concerns before starting work.
28: 
29:     ## Your Job
30: 
31:     Once you're clear on requirements:
32:     1. Implement exactly what the task specifies
33:     2. Write tests (following TDD if task says to)
34:     3. Verify implementation works
35:     4. Commit your work
36:     5. Self-review (see below)
37:     6. Report back
38: 
39:     Work from: [directory]
40: 
41:     **While you work:** If you encounter something unexpected or unclear, **ask questions**.
42:     It's always OK to pause and clarify. Don't guess or make assumptions.
43: 
44:     ## Before Reporting Back: Self-Review
45: 
46:     Review your work with fresh eyes. Ask yourself:
47: 
48:     **Completeness:**
49:     - Did I fully implement everything in the spec?
50:     - Did I miss any requirements?
51:     - Are there edge cases I didn't handle?
52: 
53:     **Quality:**
54:     - Is this my best work?
55:     - Are names clear and accurate (match what things do, not how they work)?
56:     - Is the code clean and maintainable?
57: 
58:     **Discipline:**
59:     - Did I avoid overbuilding (YAGNI)?
60:     - Did I only build what was requested?
61:     - Did I follow existing patterns in the codebase?
62: 
63:     **Testing:**
64:     - Do tests actually verify behavior (not just mock behavior)?
65:     - Did I follow TDD if required?
66:     - Are tests comprehensive?
67: 
68:     If you find issues during self-review, fix them now before reporting.
69: 
70:     ## Report Format
71: 
72:     When done, report:
73:     - What you implemented
74:     - What you tested and test results
75:     - Files changed
76:     - Self-review findings (if any)
77:     - Any issues or concerns
78:     - Discoveries: anything you found that contradicts, extends, or narrows the original ticket/spec (new requirements, constraints, complications, dependency changes, scope reductions, file changes)
79: ```

<content>1: // ─── WebSocket Store ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // Manages WebSocket connection lifecycle and centralized message dispatch.
3: // Replaces the handler registry pattern from vanilla modules.
4: 
5: import type { ConnectionStatus, RelayMessage, ToolMessage } from "../types.js";
6: import {
7: 	addUserMessage,
8: 	chatState,
9: 	clearMessages,
10: 	flushPendingRender,
11: 	handleDelta,
12: 	handleDone,
13: 	handleError,
14: 	handleMessageRemoved,
15: 	handlePartRemoved,
16: 	handleResult,
17: 	handleStatus,
18: 	handleThinkingDelta,
19: 	handleThinkingStart,
20: 	handleThinkingStop,
21: 	handleToolExecuting,
22: 	handleToolResult,
23: 	handleToolStart,
24: } from "./chat.svelte.js";
25: import {
26: 	handleAgentList,
27: 	handleCommandList,
28: 	handleModelInfo,
29: 	handleModelList,
30: } from "./discovery.svelte.js";
31: import {
32: 	handleAskUser,
33: 	handleAskUserResolved,
34: 	handlePermissionRequest,
35: 	handlePermissionResolved,
36: } from "./permissions.svelte.js";
37: import { handleProjectList } from "./project.svelte.js";
38: import { handleSessionList, handleSessionSwitched } from "./session.svelte.js";
39: import {
40: 	handlePtyCreated,
41: 	handlePtyDeleted,
42: 	handlePtyError,
43: 	handlePtyExited,
44: 	handlePtyList,
45: 	handlePtyOutput,
46: } from "./terminal.svelte.js";
47: import {
48: 	removeBanner,
49: 	setClientCount,
50: 	showBanner,
51: 	showToast,
52: } from "./ui.svelte.js";
53: 
54: // ─── Re-exports from extracted modules ──────────────────────────────────────
55: // These were extracted for modularity but consumers still import from here.
56: 
57: export {
58: 	isPushActive,
59: 	setPushActive,
60: 	triggerNotifications,
61: } from "./ws-notifications.js";
62: 
63: import { triggerNotifications } from "./ws-notifications.js";
64: 
65: export {
66: 	fileBrowserListeners,
67: 	fileHistoryListeners,
68: 	historyPageListeners,
69: 	type MessageListener,
70: 	onFileBrowser,
71: 	onFileHistory,
72: 	onHistoryPage,
73: 	onPlanMode,
74: 	onProject,
75: 	onRewind,
76: 	planModeListeners,
77: 	projectListeners,
78: 	rewindListeners,
79: } from "./ws-listeners.js";
80: 
81: import {
82: 	fileBrowserListeners,
83: 	fileHistoryListeners,
84: 	historyPageListeners,
85: 	planModeListeners,
86: 	projectListeners,
87: 	rewindListeners,
88: } from "./ws-listeners.js";
89: 
90: // ─── State ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
91: 
92: let _ws: WebSocket | null = null;
93: let _reconnectTimer: ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null = null;
94: 
95: const RECONNECT_DELAY_MS = 2000;
96: 
97: export const wsState = $state({
98: 	status: "" as ConnectionStatus,
99: 	statusText: "",
100: });
101: 
102: // ─── Derived getters ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
103: 
104: /** Get whether the WebSocket is connected. */
105: export function getIsConnected(): boolean {
106: 	return wsState.status === "connected" || wsState.status === "processing";
107: }
108: 
109: // ─── Client-side rate limiting ──────────────────────────────────────────────
110: // Mirrors server-side limits to prevent RATE_LIMITED errors.
111: 
112: const MAX_MESSAGES = 5;
113: const WINDOW_MS = 10_000;
114: 
115: /** Timestamps of recent chat message sends (within the sliding window). */
116: let _sendTimestamps: number[] = [];
117: 
118: /** Queued message waiting to be sent after the window slides. */
119: let _queuedMessage: Record<string, unknown> | null = null;
120: 
121: /** Timer for draining the queued message. */
122: let _drainTimer: ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null = null;
123: 
124: /** Clock function — overridable for testing. */
125: let _now: () => number = () => Date.now();
126: 
127: /**
128:  * Reset rate-limit state. Exported for testing only.
129:  * @internal
130:  */
131: export function _resetRateLimit(opts?: { now?: () => number }): void {
132: 	_sendTimestamps = [];
133: 	_queuedMessage = null;
134: 	if (_drainTimer) {
135: 		clearTimeout(_drainTimer);
136: 		_drainTimer = null;
137: 	}
138: 	_now = opts?.now ?? (() => Date.now());
139: }
140: 
141: /** Remove expired timestamps from the sliding window. */
142: function pruneTimestamps(): void {
143: 	const cutoff = _now() - WINDOW_MS;
144: 	_sendTimestamps = _sendTimestamps.filter((t) => t > cutoff);
145: }
146: 
147: /** Send raw data over the WebSocket (no rate limiting). */
148: function rawSend(data: Record<string, unknown>): void {
149: 	if (_ws && _ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
150: 		_ws.send(JSON.stringify(data));
151: 	}
152: }
153: 
154: /** Schedule the drain timer for the queued message. */
155: function scheduleDrain(): void {
156: 	if (_drainTimer) {
157: 		clearTimeout(_drainTimer);
158: 		_drainTimer = null;
159: 	}
160: 	if (!_queuedMessage) return;
161: 
162: 	pruneTimestamps();
163: 
164: 	// Oldest timestamp determines when the next slot opens.
165: 	// With noUncheckedIndexedAccess, _sendTimestamps[0] is number | undefined.
166: 	const oldest = _sendTimestamps[0];
167: 	const retryAfterMs = oldest !== undefined ? oldest + WINDOW_MS - _now() : 0;
168: 	const delay = Math.max(0, retryAfterMs);
169: 
170: 	_drainTimer = setTimeout(() => {
171: 		_drainTimer = null;
172: 		if (!_queuedMessage) return;
173: 
174: 		pruneTimestamps();
175: 		const msg = _queuedMessage;
176: 		_queuedMessage = null;
177: 		_sendTimestamps.push(_now());
178: 		rawSend(msg);
179: 	}, delay);
180: }
181: 
182: // ─── Core functions ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
183: 
184: /**
185:  * Send a JSON message over the WebSocket.
186:  * Chat messages (type "message") are rate-limited to match the server-side
187:  * sliding window (MAX_MESSAGES per WINDOW_MS). Non-chat control messages
188:  * are sent immediately.
189:  */
190: export function wsSend(data: Record<string, unknown>): void {
191: 	// Non-chat messages bypass rate limiting entirely.
192: 	if (data.type !== "message") {
193: 		rawSend(data);
194: 		return;
195: 	}
196: 
197: 	pruneTimestamps();
198: 
199: 	if (_sendTimestamps.length < MAX_MESSAGES) {
200: 		// Under limit — send immediately.
201: 		_sendTimestamps.push(_now());
202: 		rawSend(data);
203: 		return;
204: 	}
205: 
206: 	// At limit — queue and show feedback.
207: 	_queuedMessage = data;
208: 	showToast("Message queued — sending shortly", { variant: "warn" });
209: 	scheduleDrain();
210: }
211: 
212: /** Set connection status with tooltip text. */
213: function setStatus(status: ConnectionStatus, text: string): void {
214: 	wsState.status = status;
215: 	wsState.statusText = text;
216: }
217: 
218: // ─── Connection lifecycle ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
219: 
220: /** Connect callbacks — called after connection established. */
221: let _onConnectFn: (() => void) | null = null;
222: let _onDisconnectFn: (() => void) | null = null;
223: 
224: export function onConnect(fn: () => void): void {
225: 	_onConnectFn = fn;
226: }
227: 
228: export function onDisconnect(fn: () => void): void {
229: 	_onDisconnectFn = fn;
230: }
231: 
232: /** Current slug — stored for auto-reconnect so it reconnects to the right project. */
233: let _currentSlug: string | undefined;
234: 
235: /** Establish WebSocket connection with auto-reconnect. */
236: export function connect(slug?: string): void {
237: 	_currentSlug = slug;
238: 
239: 	if (_ws) {
240: 		// Remove event listeners to prevent the old socket's close handler
241: 		// from interfering with the new connection (race condition fix).
242: 		const oldWs = _ws;
243: 		_ws = null;
244: 		oldWs.close();
245: 	}
246: 
247: 	if (_reconnectTimer) {
248: 		clearTimeout(_reconnectTimer);
249: 		_reconnectTimer = null;
250: 	}
251: 
252: 	const protocol = window.location.protocol === "https:" ? "wss:" : "ws:";
253: 	const path = slug ? `/p/${slug}/ws` : "/ws";
254: 	const url = `${protocol}//${window.location.host}${path}`;
255: 
256: 	const ws = new WebSocket(url);
257: 	_ws = ws;
258: 
259: 	ws.addEventListener("open", () => {
260: 		// Guard: only act if this is still the current socket
261: 		if (_ws !== ws) return;
262: 		setStatus("connected", "Connected");
263: 		_onConnectFn?.();
264: 	});
265: 
266: 	ws.addEventListener("close", () => {
267: 		// Guard: only act if this is still the current socket.
268: 		// If connect() was called again, _ws points to the new socket
269: 		// and we must not null it or start a reconnect timer.
270: 		if (_ws !== ws) return;
271: 
272: 		setStatus("disconnected", "Disconnected");
273: 		_ws = null;
274: 
275: 		// Reset chat streaming/processing state so UI isn't stuck
276: 		chatState.streaming = false;
277: 		chatState.processing = false;
278: 
279: 		_onDisconnectFn?.();
280: 
281: 		// Auto-reconnect (use stored slug so we reconnect to the right project)
282: 		if (_reconnectTimer) clearTimeout(_reconnectTimer);
283: 		_reconnectTimer = setTimeout(() => {
284: 			_reconnectTimer = null;
285: 			connect(_currentSlug);
286: 		}, RECONNECT_DELAY_MS);
287: 	});
288: 
289: 	ws.addEventListener("error", () => {
290: 		if (_ws !== ws) return;
291: 		setStatus("error", "Connection error");
292: 	});
293: 
294: 	ws.addEventListener("message", (event) => {
295: 		if (_ws !== ws) return;
296: 		try {
297: 			const msg = JSON.parse(event.data) as RelayMessage;
298: 			handleMessage(msg);
299: 		} catch {
300: 			console.warn("[ws] Failed to parse message:", event.data);
301: 		}
302: 	});
303: }
304: 
305: /** Disconnect and stop reconnecting. */
306: export function disconnect(): void {
307: 	if (_reconnectTimer) {
308: 		clearTimeout(_reconnectTimer);
309: 		_reconnectTimer = null;
310: 	}
311: 	if (_ws) {
312: 		_ws.close();
313: 		_ws = null;
314: 	}
315: 	setStatus("disconnected", "Disconnected");
316: }
317: 
318: // ─── Centralized message dispatch ───────────────────────────────────────────
319: 
320: /**
321:  * Route an incoming WebSocket message to the appropriate store handler.
322:  * Replaces the vanilla handler registry pattern.
323:  */
324: export function handleMessage(msg: RelayMessage): void {
325: 	switch (msg.type) {
326: 		// ─── Chat / Streaming ────────────────────────────────────────────
327: 		case "delta":
328: 			handleDelta(msg);
329: 			break;
330: 		case "thinking_start":
331: 			handleThinkingStart(msg);
332: 			break;
333: 		case "thinking_delta":
334: 			handleThinkingDelta(msg);
335: 			break;
336: 		case "thinking_stop":
337: 			handleThinkingStop(msg);
338: 			break;
339: 		case "tool_start":
340: 			handleToolStart(msg);
341: 			break;
342: 		case "tool_executing":
343: 			handleToolExecuting(msg);
344: 			break;
345: 		case "tool_result":
346: 			handleToolResult(msg);
347: 			break;
348: 		case "tool_content":
349: 			handleToolContentResponse(msg);
350: 			break;
351: 		case "result":
352: 			handleResult(msg);
353: 			break;
354: 		case "done":
355: 			handleDone(msg);
356: 			triggerNotifications(msg);
357: 			break;
358: 		case "status":
359: 			handleStatus(msg);
360: 			break;
361: 		case "error":
362: 			handleChatError(msg);
363: 			triggerNotifications(msg);
364: 			break;
365: 
366: 		// ─── Sessions ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
367: 		case "session_list":
368: 			handleSessionList(msg);
369: 			break;
370: 		case "session_switched":
371: 			console.debug(
372: 				`[ws] session_switched id=${msg.id} events=${Array.isArray(msg.events) ? msg.events.length : "none"} history=${msg.history ? "yes" : "no"}`,
373: 			);
374: 			handleSessionSwitched(msg);
375: 			clearMessages();
376: 
377: 			// Reset HistoryView synchronously BEFORE dispatching new data.
378: 			// This replaces the $effect in HistoryView that was racing with
379: 			// handleHistoryPage (the $effect fired in a microtask AFTER data
380: 			// was loaded, wiping it).
381: 			for (const fn of historyPageListeners) {
382: 				fn({ type: "history_reset" });
383: 			}
384: 
385: 			if (msg.events) {
386: 				// Cache hit: replay raw events through existing chat handlers
387: 				// (full fidelity — same code paths as live streaming)
388: 				replayEvents(msg.events);
389: 			} else if (msg.history) {
390: 				// REST API fallback: dispatch structured messages to HistoryView
391: 				for (const fn of historyPageListeners) {
392: 					fn({
393: 						type: "history_page",
394: 						sessionId: msg.id,
395: 						messages: msg.history.messages,
396: 						hasMore: msg.history.hasMore,
397: 						total: msg.history.total,
398: 					});
399: 				}
400: 			}
401: 			break;
402: 
403: 		// ─── Terminal / PTY ──────────────────────────────────────────────
404: 		case "pty_list":
405: 			handlePtyList(msg);
406: 			break;
407: 		case "pty_created":
408: 			handlePtyCreated(msg);
409: 			break;
410: 		case "pty_output":
411: 			handlePtyOutput(msg);
412: 			break;
413: 		case "pty_exited":
414: 			handlePtyExited(msg);
415: 			break;
416: 		case "pty_deleted":
417: 			handlePtyDeleted(msg);
418: 			break;
419: 
420: 		// ─── Discovery ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
421: 		case "agent_list":
422: 			handleAgentList(msg);
423: 			break;
424: 		case "model_list":
425: 			handleModelList(msg);
426: 			break;
427: 		case "model_info":
428: 			handleModelInfo(msg);
429: 			break;
430: 		case "command_list":
431: 			handleCommandList(msg);
432: 			break;
433: 
434: 		// ─── Permissions & Questions ─────────────────────────────────────
435: 		case "permission_request":
436: 			handlePermissionRequest(msg, wsSend);
437: 			triggerNotifications(msg);
438: 			break;
439: 		case "permission_resolved":
440: 			handlePermissionResolved(msg);
441: 			break;
442: 		case "ask_user":
443: 			handleAskUser(msg);
444: 			triggerNotifications(msg);
445: 			break;
446: 		case "ask_user_resolved":
447: 			handleAskUserResolved(msg);
448: 			break;
449: 
450: 		// ─── UI ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
451: 		case "client_count":
452: 			setClientCount(msg.count ?? 0);
453: 			break;
454: 		case "connection_status":
455: 			handleConnectionStatus(msg);
456: 			break;
457: 		case "banner":
458: 		case "skip_permissions":
459: 		case "update_available":
460: 			handleBannerMessage(msg);
461: 			break;
462: 
463: 		// ─── History ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
464: 		case "history_page":
465: 			// Handled by history component directly (not stored globally)
466: 			for (const fn of historyPageListeners) fn(msg);
467: 			break;
468: 
469: 		// ─── Plan Mode ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
470: 		case "plan_enter":
471: 		case "plan_exit":
472: 		case "plan_content":
473: 		case "plan_approval":
474: 			for (const fn of planModeListeners) fn(msg);
475: 			break;
476: 
477: 		// ─── File Browser ────────────────────────────────────────────────
478: 		case "file_list":
479: 		case "file_content":
480: 			for (const fn of fileBrowserListeners) fn(msg);
481: 			break;
482: 
483: 		// ─── File Changes (routed to both browser and history) ──────────
484: 		case "file_changed":
485: 			for (const fn of fileBrowserListeners) fn(msg);
486: 			for (const fn of fileHistoryListeners) fn(msg);
487: 			break;
488: 		case "file_history_result":
489: 			for (const fn of fileHistoryListeners) fn(msg);
490: 			break;
491: 
492: 		// ─── Rewind ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
493: 		case "rewind_result":
494: 			for (const fn of rewindListeners) fn(msg);
495: 			break;
496: 
497: 		// ─── Project ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
498: 		case "project_list":
499: 			handleProjectList(msg);
500: 			for (const fn of projectListeners) fn(msg);
501: 			break;
502: 
503: 		// ─── Todo ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
504: 		// Todo messages are handled as tool_start/tool_result
505: 		// (the TodoWrite tool result contains JSON parsed by the todo component)
506: 
507: 		// ─── Part / Message removal ──────────────────────────────────────
508: 		case "part_removed":
509: 			handlePartRemoved(msg);
510: 			break;
511: 		case "message_removed":
512: 			handleMessageRemoved(msg);
513: 			break;
514: 
515: 		default:
516: 			// Unknown message type — log for debugging
517: 			// Cast needed: import.meta.env is Vite-specific, not in standard tsconfig
518: 			if ((import.meta as { env?: { DEV?: boolean } }).env?.DEV) {
519: 				console.debug("[ws] Unhandled message type:", msg.type, msg);
520: 			}
521: 			break;
522: 	}
523: }
524: 
525: // ─── Event Replay (session switch with cached events) ────────────────────────
526: // Replays raw events through existing chat handlers — identical code paths
527: // as live streaming. Zero conversion, full fidelity, handles mid-stream.
528: 
529: function replayEvents(events: RelayMessage[]): void {
530: 	chatState.replaying = true;
531: 	for (const event of events) {
532: 		switch (event.type) {
533: 			case "user_message":
534: 				addUserMessage(event.text);
535: 				break;
536: 			case "delta":
537: 				handleDelta(event);
538: 				break;
539: 			case "done":
540: 				handleDone(event);
541: 				break;
542: 			case "tool_start":
543: 				handleToolStart(event);
544: 				break;
545: 			case "tool_executing":
546: 				handleToolExecuting(event);
547: 				break;
548: 			case "tool_result":
549: 				handleToolResult(event);
550: 				break;
551: 			case "result":
552: 				handleResult(event);
553: 				break;
554: 			case "thinking_start":
555: 				handleThinkingStart(event);
556: 				break;
557: 			case "thinking_delta":
558: 				handleThinkingDelta(event);
559: 				break;
560: 			case "thinking_stop":
561: 				handleThinkingStop(event);
562: 				break;
563: 			case "status":
564: 				handleStatus(event);
565: 				break;
566: 			case "error":
567: 				handleError(event);
568: 				break;
569: 			// Skip: session_switched, permission_*, session_list, etc.
570: 			// These are not part of the chat message stream.
571: 		}
572: 	}
573: 	// Flush any pending debounced render (for mid-stream sessions
574: 	// where no "done" event has been received yet)
575: 	flushPendingRender();
576: 	chatState.replaying = false;
577: }
578: 
579: // ─── Tool content handler (full-content fetch for truncated results) ────────
580: 
581: function handleToolContentResponse(
582: 	msg: Extract<RelayMessage, { type: "tool_content" }>,
583: ): void {
584: 	const { toolId, content } = msg;
585: 	const messages = [...chatState.messages];
586: 	const idx = messages.findIndex(
587: 		(m) => m.type === "tool" && (m as ToolMessage).id === toolId,
588: 	);
589: 	if (idx >= 0) {
590: 		chatState.messages = messages.map((m, i) =>
591: 			i === idx
592: 				? {
593: 						...(m as ToolMessage),
594: 						result: content,
595: 						isTruncated: false,
596: 						fullContentLength: undefined,
597: 					}
598: 				: m,
599: 		);
600: 	}
601: }
602: 
603: // ─── Error handler (needs special routing) ──────────────────────────────────
604: 
605: function handleChatError(msg: Extract<RelayMessage, { type: "error" }>): void {
606: 	const code = msg.code;
607: 
608: 	// PTY-related errors
609: 	if (code === "PTY_CONNECT_FAILED" || code === "HANDLER_ERROR") {
610: 		handlePtyError(msg);
611: 		return;
612: 	}
613: 
614: 	// Chat errors
615: 	handleError(msg);
616: }
617: 
618: // ─── Connection status handler ──────────────────────────────────────────────
619: 
620: const CONNECTION_BANNER_ID = "opencode-connection-status";
621: 
622: function handleConnectionStatus(
623: 	msg: Extract<RelayMessage, { type: "connection_status" }>,
624: ): void {
625: 	if (msg.status === "connected") {
626: 		removeBanner(CONNECTION_BANNER_ID);
627: 	} else {
628: 		const text =
629: 			msg.status === "reconnecting"
630: 				? "Reconnecting to OpenCode…"
631: 				: "OpenCode server disconnected";
632: 		// Remove first so text updates if status changes (e.g. disconnected → reconnecting)
633: 		removeBanner(CONNECTION_BANNER_ID);
634: 		showBanner({
635: 			id: CONNECTION_BANNER_ID,
636: 			variant: "warning",
637: 			icon: "alert-triangle",
638: 			text,
639: 			dismissible: false,
640: 		});
641: 	}
642: }
643: 
644: // ─── Banner handler ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
645: 
646: function handleBannerMessage(msg: RelayMessage): void {
647: 	switch (msg.type) {
648: 		case "update_available":
649: 			showBanner({
650: 				id: "update-available",
651: 				variant: "update",
652: 				icon: "arrow-up-circle",
653: 				text: `Update available: ${msg.version ?? "new version"}`,
654: 				dismissible: true,
655: 				version: msg.version,
656: 			});
657: 			break;
658: 		case "skip_permissions":
659: 			showBanner({
660: 				id: "skip-permissions",
661: 				variant: "skip-permissions",
662: 				icon: "shield-off",
663: 				text: "Permissions are being skipped",
664: 				dismissible: true,
665: 			});
666: 			break;
667: 		case "banner":
668: 			showBanner({
669: 				id: msg.config.id ?? "custom",
670: 				variant:
671: 					(msg.config.variant as
672: 						| "update"
673: 						| "onboarding"
674: 						| "skip-permissions"
675: 						| "warning") ?? "onboarding",
676: 				icon: msg.config.icon ?? "info",
677: 				text: msg.config.text ?? "",
678: 				dismissible: msg.config.dismissible ?? true,
679: 			});
680: 			break;
681: 	}
682: }

<content>1: // ─── Client Init (Ticket 3.6) ────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // Handles the initial handshake when a browser client connects via WebSocket.
3: // Sends session info (with cached events or REST API history), model info,
4: // agent list, provider/model list, and PTY replay to the new client.
5: //
6: // Extracted from relay-stack.ts's `client_connected` handler so the logic is
7: // independently testable and relay-stack stays slim.
8: 
9: import { formatErrorDetail, RelayError } from "./errors.js";
10: import { filterAgents } from "./handlers/index.js";
11: import type { MessageCache } from "./message-cache.js";
12: import type { OpenCodeClient } from "./opencode-client.js";
13: import type { PermissionBridge } from "./permission-bridge.js";
14: import type { PtyManager } from "./pty-manager.js";
15: import type { QuestionBridge } from "./question-bridge.js";
16: import type { SessionManager } from "./session-manager.js";
17: import type { SessionOverrides } from "./session-overrides.js";
18: import type { RelayMessage } from "./types.js";
19: 
20: // ─── Dependencies ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
21: 
22: export interface ClientInitDeps {
23: 	wsHandler: {
24: 		broadcast: (msg: RelayMessage) => void;
25: 		sendTo: (clientId: string, msg: RelayMessage) => void;
26: 	};
27: 	client: OpenCodeClient;
28: 	sessionMgr: SessionManager;
29: 	messageCache: MessageCache;
30: 	overrides: SessionOverrides;
31: 	ptyManager: PtyManager;
32: 	permissionBridge: Pick<PermissionBridge, "getPending">;
33: 	questionBridge: Pick<QuestionBridge, "getPending">;
34: 	log: (...args: unknown[]) => void;
35: }
36: 
37: // ─── Handler ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
38: 
39: /**
40:  * Handle a newly connected browser client. Sends all initial state:
41:  * - Active session with cached events or REST API history
42:  * - Session list
43:  * - Model info (from session or overrides)
44:  * - Agent list (filtered)
45:  * - Provider/model list (connected only)
46:  * - PTY list + scrollback replay
47:  *
48:  * Errors are sent as INIT_FAILED messages without crashing the handler.
49:  */
50: export async function handleClientConnected(
51: 	deps: ClientInitDeps,
52: 	clientId: string,
53: ): Promise<void> {
54: 	const {
55: 		wsHandler,
56: 		client,
57: 		sessionMgr,
58: 		messageCache,
59: 		overrides,
60: 		ptyManager,
61: 		permissionBridge,
62: 		questionBridge,
63: 	} = deps;
64: 
65: 	const sendInitError = (err: unknown, prefix: string) => {
66: 		deps.log(`   [ws] ${prefix}: ${formatErrorDetail(err)}`);
67: 		wsHandler.sendTo(
68: 			clientId,
69: 			RelayError.fromCaught(err, "INIT_FAILED", prefix).toMessage(),
70: 		);
71: 	};
72: 
73: 	// ── Active session with event replay ─────────────────────────────────
74: 	const activeId = sessionMgr.getActiveSessionId();
75: 	if (activeId) {
76: 		const events = messageCache.getEvents(activeId);
77: 		const hasChatContent =
78: 			events?.some((e) => e.type === "user_message" || e.type === "delta") ??
79: 			false;
80: 		if (events && hasChatContent) {
81: 			// Cache hit with content: include raw events for client replay
82: 			wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, {
83: 				type: "session_switched",
84: 				id: activeId,
85: 				events,
86: 			});
87: 		} else {
88: 			// Cache miss (session from before relay started): REST API fallback
89: 			try {
90: 				const history = await sessionMgr.loadHistory(activeId);
91: 				wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, {
92: 					type: "session_switched",
93: 					id: activeId,
94: 					history: {
95: 						messages: history.messages,
96: 						hasMore: history.hasMore,
97: 						total: history.total,
98: 					},
99: 				});
100: 			} catch {
101: 				// If REST API fails too, just send the switch without history
102: 				wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, {
103: 					type: "session_switched",
104: 					id: activeId,
105: 				});
106: 			}
107: 		}
108: 		wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, { type: "status", status: "idle" });
109: 
110: 		// Send model/agent info from the active session
111: 		try {
112: 			const session = await client.getSession(activeId);
113: 			if (session.modelID) {
114: 				wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, {
115: 					type: "model_info",
116: 					model: session.modelID,
117: 					provider: session.providerID ?? "",
118: 				});
119: 			} else if (overrides.model) {
120: 				// Session has no model set, but relay has a selected model
121: 				wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, {
122: 					type: "model_info",
123: 					model: overrides.model.modelID,
124: 					provider: overrides.model.providerID,
125: 				});
126: 			}
127: 		} catch (err) {
128: 			sendInitError(err, "Failed to load session info");
129: 			if (overrides.model) {
130: 				wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, {
131: 					type: "model_info",
132: 					model: overrides.model.modelID,
133: 					provider: overrides.model.providerID,
134: 				});
135: 			}
136: 		}
137: 	}
138: 
139: 	// ── Session list ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
140: 	try {
141: 		const sessions = await sessionMgr.listSessions();
142: 		wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, { type: "session_list", sessions });
143: 	} catch (err) {
144: 		sendInitError(err, "Failed to list sessions");
145: 	}
146: 
147: 	// ── Pending permissions + questions (reconnect replay) ───────────────
148: 	for (const perm of permissionBridge.getPending()) {
149: 		wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, {
150: 			type: "permission_request",
151: 			requestId: perm.requestId,
152: 			toolName: perm.toolName,
153: 			toolInput: perm.toolInput,
154: 		});
155: 	}
156: 	for (const q of questionBridge.getPending()) {
157: 		wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, {
158: 			type: "ask_user",
159: 			toolId: q.toolId,
160: 			questions: q.questions,
161: 		});
162: 	}
163: 
164: 	// ── Agent list (filter out internal agents) ──────────────────────────
165: 	try {
166: 		const rawAgents = await client.listAgents();
167: 		const agents = filterAgents(rawAgents);
168: 		wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, { type: "agent_list", agents });
169: 	} catch (err) {
170: 		sendInitError(err, "Failed to list agents");
171: 	}
172: 
173: 	// ── Provider/model list + auto-select default ────────────────────────
174: 	try {
175: 		const providerResult = await client.listProviders();
176: 		const connectedSet = new Set(providerResult.connected);
177: 		const providers = providerResult.providers
178: 			.map((p) => ({
179: 				id: p.id || p.name || "",
180: 				name: p.name || p.id || "",
181: 				configured: connectedSet.has(p.id) || connectedSet.has(p.name),
182: 				models: (p.models ?? []).map((m) => ({
183: 					id: m.id,
184: 					name: m.name || m.id,
185: 					provider: p.id || p.name || "",
186: 				})),
187: 			}))
188: 			.filter((p) => p.configured);
189: 		wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, { type: "model_list", providers });
190: 
191: 		// Auto-select default model from a connected provider if none set
192: 		if (!overrides.model) {
193: 			for (const providerId of providerResult.connected) {
194: 				const defaultModelId = providerResult.defaults[providerId];
195: 				if (defaultModelId) {
196: 					overrides.setModelDefault({
197: 						providerID: providerId,
198: 						modelID: defaultModelId,
199: 					});
200: 					wsHandler.broadcast({
201: 						type: "model_info",
202: 						model: defaultModelId,
203: 						provider: providerId,
204: 					});
205: 					deps.log(
206: 						`   [model] Auto-selected default: ${defaultModelId} (${providerId})`,
207: 					);
208: 					break;
209: 				}
210: 			}
211: 		}
212: 	} catch (err) {
213: 		sendInitError(err, "Failed to list providers");
214: 	}
215: 
216: 	// ── PTY list + scrollback replay ─────────────────────────────────────
217: 	if (ptyManager.sessionCount > 0) {
218: 		const ptys = ptyManager.listSessions();
219: 		wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, { type: "pty_list", ptys });
220: 		// Replay scrollback for each PTY to this specific client
221: 		for (const { id: ptyId } of ptys) {
222: 			const scrollback = ptyManager.getScrollback(ptyId);
223: 			if (scrollback) {
224: 				wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, {
225: 					type: "pty_output",
226: 					ptyId,
227: 					data: scrollback,
228: 				});
229: 			}
230: 			const session = ptyManager.getSession(ptyId);
231: 			if (session?.exited) {
232: 				wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, {
233: 					type: "pty_exited",
234: 					ptyId,
235: 					exitCode: session.exitCode ?? 0,
236: 				});
237: 			}
238: 		}
239: 	}
240: }

(End of file - total 240 lines)
</content>

<content>1: // ─── Permission Bridge (Ticket 1.5) ─────────────────────────────────────────
2: 
3: import type {
4: 	FrontendDecision,
5: 	OpenCodeDecision,
6: 	OpenCodeEvent,
7: 	PendingPermission,
8: } from "./types.js";
9: 
10: // ─── Decision Mapping ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
11: 
12: const DECISION_MAP: Record<FrontendDecision, OpenCodeDecision> = {
13: 	allow: "once",
14: 	deny: "reject",
15: 	allow_always: "always",
16: };
17: 
18: const REVERSE_DECISION_MAP: Record<OpenCodeDecision, FrontendDecision> = {
19: 	once: "allow",
20: 	reject: "deny",
21: 	always: "allow_always",
22: };
23: 
24: /** Map frontend decision vocabulary → OpenCode vocabulary */
25: export function mapDecision(frontend: string): OpenCodeDecision | null {
26: 	return DECISION_MAP[frontend as FrontendDecision] ?? null;
27: }
28: 
29: /** Map OpenCode decision vocabulary → frontend vocabulary */
30: export function mapDecisionReverse(opencode: string): FrontendDecision | null {
31: 	return REVERSE_DECISION_MAP[opencode as OpenCodeDecision] ?? null;
32: }
33: 
34: // ─── Permission Bridge ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
35: 
36: export interface PermissionBridgeOptions {
37: 	timeoutMs?: number;
38: 	now?: () => number;
39: }
40: 
41: export class PermissionBridge {
42: 	private pending: Map<string, PendingPermission> = new Map();
43: 	private alwaysAllowed: Set<string> = new Set();
44: 	private readonly timeoutMs: number;
45: 	private readonly now: () => number;
46: 
47: 	constructor(options: PermissionBridgeOptions = {}) {
48: 		this.timeoutMs = options.timeoutMs ?? 5 * 60_000; // 5 minutes
49: 		this.now = options.now ?? Date.now;
50: 	}
51: 
52: 	/** Process an incoming permission.asked SSE event */
53: 	onPermissionRequest(event: OpenCodeEvent): PendingPermission | null {
54: 		const props = event.properties as {
55: 			id?: string;
56: 			permission?: string;
57: 			patterns?: string[];
58: 			metadata?: Record<string, unknown>;
59: 			always?: string[];
60: 		};
61: 
62: 		if (!props.id || !props.permission) return null;
63: 
64: 		// Auto-approve if tool is in the always-allowed set
65: 		if (this.alwaysAllowed.has(props.permission)) {
66: 			return null; // Caller should auto-reply
67: 		}
68: 
69: 		const entry: PendingPermission = {
70: 			requestId: props.id,
71: 			toolName: props.permission,
72: 			toolInput: {
73: 				patterns: props.patterns ?? [],
74: 				metadata: props.metadata ?? {},
75: 			},
76: 			always: props.always ?? [],
77: 			timestamp: this.now(),
78: 		};
79: 
80: 		this.pending.set(props.id, entry);
81: 		return entry;
82: 	}
83: 
84: 	/** Process a user's permission decision from the browser */
85: 	onPermissionResponse(
86: 		requestId: string,
87: 		decision: string,
88: 	): { mapped: OpenCodeDecision; toolName: string } | null {
89: 		const entry = this.pending.get(requestId);
90: 		if (!entry) return null; // Already resolved or unknown — ignore duplicate
91: 
92: 		const mapped = mapDecision(decision);
93: 		if (!mapped) return null;
94: 
95: 		// Track "always" decisions
96: 		if (mapped === "always") {
97: 			this.alwaysAllowed.add(entry.toolName);
98: 		}
99: 
100: 		this.pending.delete(requestId);
101: 		return { mapped, toolName: entry.toolName };
102: 	}
103: 
104: 	/** Handle a permission.replied SSE event (resolved from CLI) */
105: 	onPermissionReplied(requestId: string): boolean {
106: 		return this.pending.delete(requestId);
107: 	}
108: 
109: 	/** Get all pending permissions (for replay on reconnect) */
110: 	getPending(): PendingPermission[] {
111: 		return Array.from(this.pending.values());
112: 	}
113: 
114: 	/** Check if a tool is in the always-allowed set */
115: 	isAlwaysAllowed(toolName: string): boolean {
116: 		return this.alwaysAllowed.has(toolName);
117: 	}
118: 
119: 	/** Clear the always-allowed cache */
120: 	clearAlwaysAllowed(): void {
121: 		this.alwaysAllowed.clear();
122: 	}
123: 
124: 	/** Check for timed-out permissions and return their IDs */
125: 	checkTimeouts(): string[] {
126: 		const now = this.now();
127: 		const timedOut: string[] = [];
128: 
129: 		for (const [id, entry] of this.pending) {
130: 			if (now - entry.timestamp >= this.timeoutMs) {
131: 				timedOut.push(id);
132: 				this.pending.delete(id);
133: 			}
134: 		}
135: 
136: 		return timedOut;
137: 	}
138: 
139: 	/** Recover pending permissions from REST API response */
140: 	recoverPending(
141: 		permissions: Array<{
142: 			id: string;
143: 			permission: string;
144: 			patterns?: string[];
145: 			metadata?: Record<string, unknown>;
146: 			always?: string[];
147: 		}>,
148: 	): PendingPermission[] {
149: 		const recovered: PendingPermission[] = [];
150: 		for (const p of permissions) {
151: 			const entry: PendingPermission = {
152: 				requestId: p.id,
153: 				toolName: p.permission,
154: 				toolInput: {
155: 					patterns: p.patterns ?? [],
156: 					metadata: p.metadata ?? {},
157: 				},
158: 				always: p.always ?? [],
159: 				timestamp: this.now(),
160: 			};
161: 			this.pending.set(p.id, entry);
162: 			recovered.push(entry);
163: 		}
164: 		return recovered;
165: 	}
166: 
167: 	/** Number of pending permissions */
168: 	get size(): number {
169: 		return this.pending.size;
170: 	}
171: }

(End of file - total 171 lines)

<content>1: // ─── Question / Ask-User Bridge (Ticket 1.6) ────────────────────────────────
2: 
3: import type { AskUserQuestion, OpenCodeEvent } from "./types.js";
4: 
5: export interface PendingQuestion {
6: 	toolId: string;
7: 	questions: AskUserQuestion[];
8: 	timestamp: number;
9: }
10: 
11: export interface QuestionBridgeOptions {
12: 	timeoutMs?: number;
13: 	now?: () => number;
14: }
15: 
16: /**
17:  * Convert browser answer format to OpenCode's format.
18:  * Browser: { "0": "Option A", "1": "Option B" }
19:  * OpenCode: [["Option A"], ["Option B"]]
20:  *
21:  * For multi-select:
22:  * Browser: { "0": "Opt A, Opt B" }
23:  * OpenCode: [["Opt A", "Opt B"]]
24:  */
25: export function convertAnswers(
26: 	answers: Record<string, string>,
27: 	questions: AskUserQuestion[],
28: ): string[][] {
29: 	const result: string[][] = [];
30: 
31: 	for (let i = 0; i < questions.length; i++) {
32: 		const key = String(i);
33: 		const raw = answers[key];
34: 
35: 		if (raw === undefined || raw === "") {
36: 			result.push([]);
37: 			continue;
38: 		}
39: 
40: 		if (questions[i]!.multiSelect) {
41: 			// Split comma-separated selections
42: 			result.push(
43: 				raw
44: 					.split(",")
45: 					.map((s) => s.trim())
46: 					.filter((s) => s.length > 0),
47: 			);
48: 		} else {
49: 			result.push([raw]);
50: 		}
51: 	}
52: 
53: 	return result;
54: }
55: 
56: /**
57:  * Map OpenCode question format (with `multiple` field) to frontend format (with `multiSelect` field).
58:  */
59: export function mapQuestionFields(
60: 	ocQuestions: Array<{
61: 		question?: string;
62: 		header?: string;
63: 		options?: Array<{ label?: string; description?: string }>;
64: 		multiple?: boolean;
65: 		custom?: boolean;
66: 	}>,
67: ): AskUserQuestion[] {
68: 	return ocQuestions.map((q) => ({
69: 		question: q.question ?? "",
70: 		header: q.header ?? "",
71: 		options: (q.options ?? []).map((o) => ({
72: 			label: o.label ?? "",
73: 			description: o.description ?? "",
74: 		})),
75: 		multiSelect: q.multiple ?? false,
76: 		custom: q.custom ?? true,
77: 	}));
78: }
79: 
80: export class QuestionBridge {
81: 	private pending: Map<string, PendingQuestion> = new Map();
82: 	private readonly timeoutMs: number;
83: 	private readonly now: () => number;
84: 
85: 	constructor(options: QuestionBridgeOptions = {}) {
86: 		this.timeoutMs = options.timeoutMs ?? 10 * 60_000; // 10 minutes
87: 		this.now = options.now ?? Date.now;
88: 	}
89: 
90: 	/** Process an incoming question.asked SSE event */
91: 	onQuestion(event: OpenCodeEvent): PendingQuestion | null {
92: 		const props = event.properties as {
93: 			id?: string;
94: 			questions?: Array<{
95: 				question?: string;
96: 				header?: string;
97: 				options?: Array<{ label?: string; description?: string }>;
98: 				multiple?: boolean;
99: 				custom?: boolean;
100: 			}>;
101: 		};
102: 
103: 		if (!props.id || !props.questions) return null;
104: 
105: 		const questions = mapQuestionFields(props.questions);
106: 		const entry: PendingQuestion = {
107: 			toolId: props.id,
108: 			questions,
109: 			timestamp: this.now(),
110: 		};
111: 
112: 		this.pending.set(props.id, entry);
113: 		return entry;
114: 	}
115: 
116: 	/** Process a user's answer from the browser */
117: 	onAnswer(
118: 		toolId: string,
119: 		answers: Record<string, string>,
120: 	): { formatted: string[][] } | null {
121: 		const entry = this.pending.get(toolId);
122: 		if (!entry) return null;
123: 
124: 		const formatted = convertAnswers(answers, entry.questions);
125: 		this.pending.delete(toolId);
126: 		return { formatted };
127: 	}
128: 
129: 	/** Get all pending questions (for replay on reconnect) */
130: 	getPending(): PendingQuestion[] {
131: 		return Array.from(this.pending.values());
132: 	}
133: 
134: 	/** Check for timed-out questions */
135: 	checkTimeouts(): string[] {
136: 		const now = this.now();
137: 		const timedOut: string[] = [];
138: 
139: 		for (const [id, entry] of this.pending) {
140: 			if (now - entry.timestamp >= this.timeoutMs) {
141: 				timedOut.push(id);
142: 				// NOTE: per spec, questions do NOT auto-answer on timeout
143: 				// but we track them for notification purposes
144: 			}
145: 		}
146: 
147: 		return timedOut;
148: 	}
149: 
150: 	/** Remove a question (e.g., after timeout notification) */
151: 	remove(toolId: string): boolean {
152: 		return this.pending.delete(toolId);
153: 	}
154: 
155: 	get size(): number {
156: 		return this.pending.size;
157: 	}
158: }

(End of file - total 158 lines)
